join_order.notes: Build a dense adsh_code -> filtered_filing_gid array from sub_fy_postings and sub_adsh_dense_lookup, then probe it during one sequential num scan.

data_structures.dim_filter: direct_array(adsh_code_to_filing_gid, invalid=-1) plus filing_gid_to_outer_gid and filing_gid_to_inner_gid built from sub_fy_postings + sub_adsh_dense_lookup
data_structures.aggregation: direct_array(thread_local_filing_sums pre_sized to filtered filings, then roll up reduced filing totals into inner and outer direct arrays)

optimization_notes: Root cause: the current 65 ms main scan is dominated by a low-value postings-driven fact pass over 33.2M USD rowids and by doing outer-group and inner-group updates directly in the hot loop. That plan keeps an extra 151 MB rowid stream in flight and uses per-thread aggregate state of roughly 325 KB, which spills beyond a 256 KB L2. Strategy Category A: replace the postings-driven probe with one fused sequential scan of num(uom, adsh, value), build a dense adsh_code->filtered_filing_gid map once, aggregate each qualifying fact row first into a filing-granularity direct array, then roll up the reduced filing totals into cik and (name,cik) groups after the scan. This removes the rowid indirection, cuts hot-loop updates from four arrays to one cache-resident array, and keeps join/filter/aggregation fully branch-light and bandwidth-efficient.
